entries.rationale: Pre-W2 handlers typed `req: Request` (express raw). Stream 1 PR6 ships the scanner to halt new drift; this file is on the W4 cleanup list. Body usage of req.user / req.headers must be migrated to AuthenticatedRequest narrowing as part of that cleanup.

OAuth2 discovery + token-exchange handlers use raw express Request to access request URL + headers. Migration to typed shape requires confirming no caller depends on bare Request semantics. Tracked W4.
Pre-W2 api-key endpoints typed req: any to access req.user.userId before the typed RequestContext landed. W4 cleanup migrates each handler to UserRequestContext after assertUserContext narrowing.

Service-token mint endpoint uses raw Request to inspect bootstrap headers (X-Service-Id, X-Bootstrap-Secret, Authorization). Migration deferred — these are pre-JWT-validation reads, the typed RequestContext is not populated yet. W4.
Pre-W2 session handlers typed req: any. W4 cleanup migrates to AuthenticatedRequest.
SSO callbacks use raw Request for session-cookie + redirect URL access. Migration deferred — these are pre-JWT-validation paths. W4.
Pre-W2 iam handlers typed req: any to read req.user.userId. W4 cleanup migrates to UserRequestContext.
Pre-W2 UI handlers typed req: any. W4 cleanup migrates to AuthenticatedRequest.
Pre-W2 collection handlers typed req: any. W4 cleanup migrates to AuthenticatedRequest.
Pack install endpoints use raw Request to read X-Install-Token header + pack-control-plane delivery metadata. Migration depends on PackInstallGuard typing work. W4.
Pre-W2 property handlers typed req: any. W4 cleanup migrates to AuthenticatedRequest.

Control-plane auth handlers typed req: any for login/refresh/logout endpoints. Stream 1 PR3 + PR4 left these unchanged; W4 cleanup migrates the control-plane to its own ControlPlaneRequestContext shape.
